{"id":"TGR-x8a","title":"Spinors: Implement spinor display (show, to_latex, to_unicode) with dotted notation","description":"## Summary

Extend the display layer (show.jl) to render spinor indices with standard notation:
- Undotted (:SL2C) indices: uppercase Latin A,B,C,... in show/unicode, same in LaTeX
- Dotted (:SL2C_dot) indices: uppercase Latin with dot/prime: A',B',C' in show,
  \dot{A},\dot{B} in LaTeX, A',B' in unicode

The display must distinguish spinor from tensor indices visually, and dotted from
undotted spinor indices.

## Functions to modify

```julia
# src/show.jl

# Extend Base.show(io, idx::TIndex):
# If idx.vbundle == :SL2C_dot, append prime mark to name in display.
# Convention: stored name is :Ap, displayed as A' (strip trailing 'p', add prime)
# Or: stored name is :A, vbundle determines display -- PREFERRED (no name mangling)
#
# CHOSEN: Names stored as :A, :B for :SL2C and :A, :B for :SL2C_dot.
# Display layer adds prime based on vbundle. This means the same Symbol :A
# appears in both bundles, but TIndex equality includes vbundle so they are
# distinct. However, fresh_index must generate distinct names per bundle to
# avoid display ambiguity (e.g., :A for SL2C, :Ap for SL2C_dot in the AST).
#
# REVISED DECISION: Use distinct name symbols (:A vs :Ap) in the AST to avoid
# confusion in intermediate representations, but display :Ap as A' (strip 'p').

# to_latex(idx::TIndex): 
#   :SL2C -> "^{A}" or "_{A}" 
#   :SL2C_dot -> "^{\\dot{A}}" or "_{\\dot{A}}"

# to_unicode(idx::TIndex):
#   :SL2C -> "^A" or "_A"
#   :SL2C_dot -> "^A'" or "_A'" (using ASCII prime)
```

## Display examples

```
# show:
Tensor(:psi, [spin_down(:A)])           ->  psi[-A]
Tensor(:chi, [spin_dot_down(:Ap)])      ->  chi[-A']
Tensor(:sigma, [up(:a), spin_down(:A), spin_dot_down(:Ap)])  ->  sigma[a, -A, -A']

# to_latex:
psi^A chi_A   ->  \\psi^{A} \\chi_{A}
phi_{A'}      ->  \\phi_{\\dot{A}}

# to_unicode:
psi^A chi_A   ->  psi^A chi_A
phi_{A'}      ->  phi_A'
```

## Ground truth

- Standard notation from Penrose & Rindler Vol 1: primed/dotted indices
- xAct/Spinors uses OverDot[] for display of primed indices
- Wald Ch 13 uses primed notation: A', B'

## Tests

1. show(io, spin_down(:A)) produces "-A" (not "-A:SL2C")
2. show(io, spin_dot_down(:Ap)) produces "-A'" (converts :Ap to A')
3. to_latex(spin_dot_down(:Ap)) produces "_{\\dot{A}}"
4. to_latex of mixed tensor: T^a_{AA'} renders correctly
5. to_unicode of dotted index uses prime: A'
6. Round-trip: parse_tex of spinor LaTeX (future, not in scope, but design should not preclude)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-15T10:22:57Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-18T20:01:33Z"}

{"id":"TGR-7n2","title":"Spinors: Implement see-saw contraction rule for antisymmetric spin metric","description":"## Summary

Implement the spinor "see-saw" identity for raising/lowering spinor indices
via the antisymmetric spin metric epsilon_{AB}. Unlike the symmetric tensor
metric g_{ab}, the antisymmetric epsilon requires careful sign tracking:

  phi^A = epsilon^{AB} phi_B       (raise: use FIRST index of epsilon^{AB})
  phi_A = phi^B epsilon_{BA}       (lower: use SECOND index of epsilon_{BA})
  
The see-saw identity: psi^A chi_A = -psi_A chi^A (sign flip on contraction).
This is the signature feature of 2-component spinor calculus.

## Function

```julia
# In src/spinors/contraction.jl (~100 LOC)

contract_spin_metrics(expr::TensorExpr; registry=current_registry()) -> TensorExpr
```

Extends the existing contract_metrics() pipeline. When a spin metric epsilon^{AB}
or epsilon_{AB} appears in a product and shares a dummy spinor index with another
factor, contract it by raising/lowering the spinor index on the partner tensor.

The sign convention matters:
- epsilon^{AB} T_{B...} -> T^{A}_{...}  (no extra sign: contracted on second index of epsilon)
- epsilon^{AB} T_{A...} -> -T^{B}_{...} (extra minus: contracted on first index, antisymmetry gives sign)
- Equivalently: epsilon^{BA} T_{A...} -> T^{B}_{...} (swap first, pick up -1, then contract second)

## Integration with simplify pipeline

Option 1: Modify contract_metrics() to also handle spin metrics (check is_metric
+ antisymmetric flag). This is clean since the existing engine already handles
metric contraction.

Option 2: Separate function called after contract_metrics in the simplify pipeline.

CHOSEN: Option 1 -- extend contract_metrics. The existing _try_metric_contraction
already works for any metric tensor. The only change needed is tracking the
antisymmetry sign when the contraction is on the "wrong" index of an antisymmetric
metric (first index instead of second, or vice versa).

Specifically: when metric g has AntiSymmetric(1,2), and the contracted index is
at metric slot 1 (not slot 2), introduce an extra -1 sign.

## Ground truth

- Penrose & Rindler Vol 1 (1984), Eq 2.5.25-2.5.27:
  psi^A = epsilon^{AB} psi_B
  psi_A = psi^B epsilon_{BA}  (note: second index of epsilon)
  KEY: psi^A chi_A = epsilon^{AB} psi_B chi_A = -psi_B chi^B = -psi_A chi^A

- Wald Ch 13, Eq 13.1.14-13.1.16

## Tests

1. epsilon^{AB} phi_B simplifies to phi^A (contraction on second index)
2. epsilon^{AB} phi_A simplifies to -phi^B (contraction on first index, antisymmetry sign)
3. See-saw: simplify(psi^A chi_A + psi_A chi^A) = 0  (they cancel due to sign)
4. Double raise: epsilon^{AB} epsilon^{CD} T_{BD} simplifies correctly
5. Dotted see-saw: same tests with :SL2C_dot indices
6. Mixed: epsilon^{AB} T_{aB} (tensor + spinor indices) contracts spinor only","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-15T10:22:31Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-18T20:00:26Z"}

{"id":"TGR-6cn","title":"Spinors: Extend xperm canonicalization for spinor index symmetries","description":"## Summary

Extend the Butler-Portugal canonicalization engine (canonicalize.jl + wrapper.jl)
to correctly handle spinor index groups. The key changes are:

1. **Index group separation**: xperm must treat :SL2C, :SL2C_dot, and :Tangent
   indices as INDEPENDENT groups. Currently _canonicalize_product lumps all indices
   into a single permutation domain. For spinor expressions, indices from different
   vbundles must not be permuted into each other.

2. **Antisymmetric metric for dummies**: When spinor dummy pairs are passed to
   xperm_canonical_perm, metricQ must be -1 (antisymmetric), not +1 (symmetric).
   This affects the canonical form: X_{AB} Y^{BA} = -X_{AB} Y^{AB} under
   antisymmetric metric, vs +X_{AB} Y^{AB} under symmetric metric.

3. **Mixed tensor-spinor products**: For expressions like T_{a}^{AA'}, the
   permutation domain includes slots from all three vbundles. Symmetry generators
   must only swap slots within the same vbundle.

## Functions to modify

```julia
# src/algebra/canonicalize.jl

# _canonicalize_product(p::TProduct): Group slots by vbundle before building
# the permutation. Each vbundle group gets its own set of dummy pairs and
# free indices. The joint permutation domain includes all groups but generators
# are restricted to intra-group swaps.
#
# Key change in name_slots grouping:
#   Current:  key = (idx.name, idx.vbundle)  -- already vbundle-aware for pairing
#   Needed:   Additionally pass metricQ per vbundle group to xperm
#
# For the all-free mode (current approach), this mostly works already because
# indices are grouped by (name, vbundle). The main change is ensuring that
# symmetry generators do not cross vbundle boundaries.

# src/xperm/wrapper.jl

# xperm_canonical_perm: May need multiple calls (one per vbundle group) or
# a single call with vbundle-aware metricQ. The C API canonical_perm supports
# only one metricQ value. Options:
#   (a) Single call with metricQ=0 (no metric dummies), handle metric
#       contraction separately -- PREFERRED, matches current all-free mode
#   (b) Multiple calls per vbundle group -- complex, ordering issues
#
# CHOSEN: Option (a). Continue using all-free mode. Spinor metric contraction
# (epsilon contraction) is handled by contract_metrics, not by xperm.
```

## Design constraint

The current all-free xperm mode already avoids metricQ issues: all indices are
treated as free, and dummy normalization happens in collect_terms. This approach
extends naturally to spinors. The main work is ensuring that:
- Symmetry generators (from symmetry_generators()) respect vbundle boundaries
- Factor sort key accounts for spinor index vbundles
- _ImplodedObject handles mixed-vbundle tensors

## Ground truth

- Penrose & Rindler Vol 1 (1984), Section 3.3: spinor symmetries
- Psi_{ABCD} has FullySymmetric(1,2,3,4) on its :SL2C indices
- Phi_{ABA'B'} has Symmetric(1,2) on :SL2C pair + Symmetric(3,4) on :SL2C_dot pair
- R_{abcd} in spinor form: Psi_{ABCD} eps_{A'B'} eps_{C'D'} + ... has symmetries
  that mix tensor and spinor indices via the soldering form, NOT directly

## Tests

1. Psi_{ABCD} = Psi_{BACD} after canonicalize (symmetry within :SL2C)
2. X_{AB} Y^{BA} canonicalizes to -X_{AB} Y^{AB} (antisymmetry of epsilon contraction)
3. Phi_{ABA'B'} = Phi_{BAA'B'} (undotted pair symmetry)
4. Phi_{ABA'B'} = Phi_{ABB'A'} (dotted pair symmetry)
5. Mixed: T_{a}^{A} sigma^{BB'}_{b} -- tensor indices :Tangent not swapped with spinor :SL2C
6. Zero detection: epsilon_{AB} psi^A psi^B = 0 (antisymmetry + identical factors)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-15T10:21:38Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-18T20:00:23Z"}

{"id":"TGR-ozc","title":"Spinors: Implement soldering form sigma^a_{AA'} and tensor-spinor conversion","description":"## Summary

Implement the soldering form (Infeld-van der Waerden symbols) sigma^a_{AA'} that
provides the isomorphism between the tangent bundle and the tensor product of the
two spin bundles: V_a <-> V_{AA'} via V_{AA'} = sigma^a_{AA'} V_a.

The soldering form has 3 types of indices: one :Tangent (Up), one :SL2C (Down),
one :SL2C_dot (Down). It satisfies fundamental identities that encode the metric
and spin metric in terms of each other.

## Tensor registration

```julia
# In src/spinors/soldering.jl (~120 LOC)

define_soldering_form!(reg::TensorRegistry; manifold::Symbol=:M4, metric::Symbol=:g)
```

Registers :sigma with mixed indices:
- rank specification needs extension: currently rank=(p,q) counts Up/Down on Tangent.
  For mixed vbundle tensors, rank should be a Dict or the existing (p,q) extended.
  DECISION: Keep rank=(1,2) with options[:index_vbundles] = [:Tangent, :SL2C, :SL2C_dot]
  specifying the vbundle of each slot. This metadata is needed by canonicalize.

The soldering form has no slot symmetries (sigma^a_{AA'} != sigma^a_{A'A} because
the two down indices are on DIFFERENT vbundles and thus not interchangeable).

## Conversion functions

```julia
# tensor_to_spinor(expr, idx::Symbol; sigma=:sigma) -> TensorExpr
# Replace tensor index :a with spinor pair :AA' by inserting sigma:
#   T_a -> sigma^a_{AA'} T_a  (contracts tensor index, introduces spinor pair)
#   More precisely: T_{...a...} -> sigma^a_{AA'} T_{...a...}
#                                 = T_{...AA'...} (after contraction)

# spinor_to_tensor(expr, idx_undotted::Symbol, idx_dotted::Symbol; sigma=:sigma)
# Replace spinor pair (A, A') with tensor index by inserting sigma inverse:
#   T_{AA'} -> sigma_{AA'}^a T_{AA'} = T_a

# spinor_equivalent(expr; sigma=:sigma) -> TensorExpr
# Convert ALL tensor indices to spinor pairs. E.g.:
#   g_{ab} -> sigma^a_{AA'} sigma^b_{BB'} g_{ab} = epsilon_{AB} epsilon_{A'B'}
```

## Key identities (rules to register)

1. sigma^a_{AA'} sigma_{a}^{BB'} = epsilon^B_A epsilon^{B'}_{A'} = delta^B_A delta^{B'}_{A'}
   (completeness on spinor side)
2. sigma^a_{AA'} sigma^{b AA'} = g^{ab}  (equivalently: delta^a_b on tensor side)
3. g_{ab} = sigma_a^{AA'} sigma_{b AA'}  (metric from soldering form)
4. epsilon_{AB} epsilon_{A'B'} = sigma^a_{AA'} sigma_{a BB'}  (spin metrics from soldering)

## Ground truth

- Penrose & Rindler Vol 1 (1984), Eq 3.1.20, 3.1.23, 3.1.39
- Wald Ch 13, Eq 13.1.17-13.1.22
- Convention: sigma^a_{AA'} (tensor index up, both spinor indices down)
  with sigma_{a}^{AA'} obtained by raising/lowering

## Tests

1. sigma^a_{AA'} sigma_{a}^{BB'} simplifies to delta^B_A delta^{B'}_{A'}
2. sigma^a_{AA'} sigma^{b AA'} simplifies to g^{ab} (or delta^a_b)
3. tensor_to_spinor of V_a produces expression with spinor indices A, A'
4. spinor_to_tensor inverts tensor_to_spinor (round-trip identity)
5. Index counting: sigma has 1 Tangent + 1 SL2C + 1 SL2C_dot = 3 total slots","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-15T10:22:04Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-18T20:01:11Z"}

Provide a one-liner macro that sets up the complete spinor infrastructure on a
manifold, analogous to @manifold + define_metric! for the tensor case. This is
the user-facing entry point that chains together all the spinor registration
steps.

define_spinor_structure!(reg::TensorRegistry; manifold::Symbol=:M4, metric::Symbol=:g)
```

Calls in order:
1. define_spinor_bundles!(reg; manifold)       -- :SL2C, :SL2C_dot VBundles
2. define_spin_metric!(reg; manifold)          -- epsilon_{AB}, epsilon_{A'B'}, deltas
3. define_soldering_form!(reg; manifold, metric) -- sigma^a_{AA'}
4. Registers fundamental identities as rewrite rules

# Macro form:
@spinor_manifold M4 metric=g
# Expands to: define_spinor_structure!(current_registry(); manifold=:M4, metric=:g)
```

## Rewrite rules registered

1. sigma^a_{AA'} sigma_{a}^{BB'} -> delta^B_A delta^{B'}_{A'}
2. sigma^a_{AA'} sigma^{b AA'} -> g^{ab}

Requires manifold and metric already registered (via @manifold or define_metric!).
Errors if :SL2C already registered on a different manifold.

{"id":"TGR-wgy","title":"Design: Frame index VBundle for tetrad/Lorentz indices","description":"DESIGN DECISION: Tetrad/frame indices must live in a separate VBundle from coordinate (Tangent) indices.

Current state: TIndex has vbundle::Symbol (default :Tangent). BasisProperties has basis_type::Symbol (:coordinate, :tetrad, :orthonormal). VBundles are registered via define_vbundle!.

Design:
1. Register a :Lorentz VBundle (dim=d) for frame indices I,J,K,... separate from :Tangent for coordinate indices mu,nu,...
2. For null (NP) tetrads, the same :Lorentz VBundle works -- the null vs orthonormal distinction is in the frame metric eta_{IJ}, not the index type.
3. The tetrad e^a_I is a mixed tensor: one :Tangent Up index, one :Lorentz Down index.
4. Frame metric eta_{IJ} has two :Lorentz indices; spacetime metric g_{mu nu} has two :Tangent indices.
5. Canonicalization via xperm already handles multi-VBundle setups -- indices in different VBundles don't contract.

Convention (following xCoba): use lowercase Latin a,b,c for :Lorentz frame indices and Greek mu,nu,rho for :Tangent coordinate indices. In code: up(:a, :Lorentz) vs up(:mu) or up(:mu, :Tangent).

This unifies orthonormal and null tetrads: both use :Lorentz VBundle, distinguished by frame_metric being diag(-1,1,1,1) vs the NP null metric.

References: xCoba documentation (DefVBundle for frame indices); Chandrasekhar Ch 1 (index conventions); Stephani et al. Ch 3 (frame formalism).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-15T16:20:02Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-18T20:01:31Z"}

{"id":"TGR-d42","title":"EFTofPNG: Implement tensor contraction engine for Feynman diagrams","description":"Implement automated tensor contraction engine for Feynman diagrams in gravitational EFT.

## Background

A Feynman diagram in the EFT of gravity consists of:
- Internal lines: graviton propagators D^{abcd}(q_i)
- Vertices: n-graviton vertices V^(n) and matter-graviton couplings
- External lines: on-shell graviton polarizations or matter sources

The contraction engine connects these building blocks by:
1. Identifying internal vs external index groups at each vertex
2. Contracting propagator indices D^{abcd} with vertex index slots
3. Imposing momentum conservation at each vertex
4. Summing over internal momenta (for loop diagrams, deferred to TGR-xex)
5. Producing the resulting tensor structure in external indices + momenta

Reference: Goldberger & Rothstein hep-th/0409156, Sec 4-5; Porto 1104.2712, Sec 3.

## Design: FeynmanDiagram type

```julia
# A single line (propagator) connecting two vertices
struct DiagramLine
    propagator::TensorExpr        # e.g., graviton_propagator(...)
    momentum::Symbol              # loop or external momentum label
    vertex_a::Int                 # index of first vertex
    vertex_b::Int                 # index of second vertex
    slot_a::Int                   # which index group at vertex_a
    slot_b::Int                   # which index group at vertex_b
end

# A vertex in the diagram
struct DiagramVertex
    vertex_expr::TensorExpr       # the vertex tensor expression
    n_slots::Int                  # number of index groups (legs)
    momenta::Vector{Symbol}       # momentum at each leg
    is_matter::Bool               # matter vertex vs pure graviton
end

# The complete diagram
struct FeynmanDiagram
    vertices::Vector{DiagramVertex}
    lines::Vector{DiagramLine}
    external_legs::Vector{@NamedTuple{vertex::Int, slot::Int, momentum::Symbol}}
    loop_momenta::Vector{Symbol}  # integration variables
end
```

## Integration with existing infrastructure

Must build on:
- `contract_metrics` from `src/algebra/contraction.jl` — the core index contraction engine
- `all_contractions` from `src/algebra/all_contractions.jl` — exhaustive contraction enumeration
- `simplify` pipeline from `src/algebra/simplify.jl` — fixed-point simplification
- `canonicalize` from `src/algebra/canonicalize.jl` — Butler-Portugal for index canonicalization
- `contract_momenta` from `src/action/kernel_extraction.jl` — k_a k^a -> k^2
- `ensure_no_dummy_clash` from `src/ast/indices.jl` — dummy index management

## Function signatures

```julia
# Build a diagram from vertices and connectivity
function build_diagram(vertices::Vector{DiagramVertex},
                       edges::Vector{Tuple{Int,Int,Int,Int}};
                       external_momenta::Vector{Symbol}=Symbol[]) -> FeynmanDiagram

# Contract all internal lines in a diagram
function contract_diagram(diagram::FeynmanDiagram;
                          registry::TensorRegistry=current_registry(),
                          simplify_intermediate::Bool=true) -> TensorExpr

# Contract a single propagator with two vertices
function contract_line(prop::TensorExpr, v1::TensorExpr, v2::TensorExpr,
                       idx_group_1::Vector{TIndex}, idx_group_2::Vector{TIndex};
                       registry=current_registry()) -> TensorExpr

# Impose momentum conservation at each vertex
function impose_momentum_conservation!(diagram::FeynmanDiagram) -> FeynmanDiagram

# Identify loop momenta from topology
function find_loop_momenta(diagram::FeynmanDiagram) -> Vector{Symbol}

# Symmetry factor of the diagram
function symmetry_factor(diagram::FeynmanDiagram) -> Rational{Int}
```

## Ground truth (verification tests)

1. **Tree-level graviton exchange**: Two matter vertices connected by one propagator (single internal line, no loops). Contraction must produce:
   M_tree = (kappa^2 / 2) T1^{ab} D_{abcd}(k) T2^{cd}
   = (kappa^2 / 4k^2) [2 T1^{ab} T2_{ab} - T1^a_a T2^b_b]
   Fourier transform gives Newton potential V = -G m1 m2 / r.
   Reference: Goldberger & Rothstein hep-th/0409156, Eq 30.

2. **Tree-level 2-to-2 graviton scattering**: s + t + u channel diagrams via 3-graviton vertex. Sum over channels must be gauge-invariant (k_a M^{abcd} = 0). Reference: DeWitt vertex Feynman rules.

3. **Triangle diagram topology**: 3 vertices, 3 internal lines, 1 loop momentum. Verify correct momentum routing: q flows in loop, k1, k2 external, with conservation at each vertex.

4. **Symmetry factors**: Self-energy diagram (bubble with 2 propagators) has symmetry factor 1/2. Tadpole has 1/1. Verify against Goldberger & Rothstein counting.

## Implementation notes

- The key challenge is dummy index management: each contraction introduces new dummy pairs. Use `ensure_no_dummy_clash` and `fresh_index` from the AST layer.
- Momentum routing: for L loops and E external legs, there are L independent loop momenta. Use momentum conservation to eliminate (V-1) momenta (V = number of vertices).
- For tree diagrams (L=0), the result is a rational function of external momenta.
- For loop diagrams (L>=1), the result is a MomentumIntegral object (see TGR-xex).
- ~120 LOC in src/feynman/contraction.jl","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:31:36Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-15T10:47:55Z"}

{"id":"TGR-bgl.13","title":"xPPN: Implement PPN-to-component bridge connecting abstract tensors to 3+1 coordinate expressions","description":"Implement the bridge between abstract tensor expressions and the 3+1 component representation required by the PPN formalism. The PPN metric is defined component-by-component (g_{00}, g_{0i}, g_{ij}) with explicit v/c order assignments, while the existing codebase primarily works with abstract indexed tensors. This issue provides the conversion layer.

Key design:
- ppn_foliation!(reg; manifold=:M4, temporal=0, spatial=[1,2,3]) defines the 3+1 split
  and registers spatial delta, spatial metric, lapse, shift as appropriate for PPN
- Uses existing FoliationProperties from src/foliation/foliation.jl
- ppn_decompose(g_ab) -> (g_00::TensorExpr, g_0i::TensorExpr, g_ij::TensorExpr) splits the metric
- ppn_compose(g_00, g_0i, g_ij) -> g_ab reconstructs the full metric
- Christoffel components: Gamma^a_{bc} auto-computed from g_{00}, g_{0i}, g_{ij} at each v/c order
  using existing metric_christoffel infrastructure from src/components/metric_compute.jl
- Ricci components: Ric_{ab} at each order, using the PPN-truncated Christoffels

Integration points:
1. src/foliation/foliation.jl -- FoliationProperties, split_spacetime, split_all_spacetime
2. src/components/metric_compute.jl -- metric_christoffel, metric_riemann, metric_ricci
3. src/components/ctensor.jl -- CTensor arithmetic for component expressions
4. src/perturbation/backgrounds.jl -- vacuum_background! / maximally_symmetric_background!

Design constraints:
- The PPN expansion is NOT a standard perturbation (no small parameter epsilon). It is an
  asymptotic expansion in v/c where different metric components have different leading orders.
  This means MetricPerturbation from metric_perturbation.jl is NOT directly usable.
- Must handle the order mismatch: g_{00} = -1 + O(2) + O(4), g_{0i} = O(3), g_{ij} = delta_{ij} + O(2)
- Time derivatives introduce additional v/c factors: partial_0 f ~ (v/c) partial_i f for bound systems
- The spatial Laplacian delta^{ij} partial_i partial_j is NOT the spacetime d'Alembertian

Add to src/ppn/decompose.jl.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Ch 4.1; MTW Ch 39.

Acceptance:
- ppn_decompose splits g_{ab} into (g_00, g_0i, g_ij) components
- Christoffel components computed order-by-order agree with Will (2018) Eqs 4.13-4.19
- Integration with existing foliation infrastructure is seamless (uses FoliationProperties)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-15T16:20:57Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-15T16:20:57Z"}

{"id":"TGR-xex","title":"EFTofPNG: Implement loop integral representation with d-dimensional regularization","description":"Implement representation and reduction of loop integrals with d-dimensional regularization for gravitational EFT.

## Background

One-loop graviton exchange diagrams in NRGR produce momentum integrals of the form:

    I = int d^d q / (2 pi)^d  N(q, k_ext) / [q^2 (q - k1)^2 (q - k2)^2 ...]

where N is a tensor-valued numerator (polynomial in loop momentum q and external momenta k_i), and the denominator contains propagator poles. These integrals require:

1. **d-dimensional regularization**: spacetime dimension d = 4 - 2 epsilon, with poles in 1/epsilon manifesting UV divergences
2. **Passarino-Veltman reduction**: tensor integrals reduced to scalar master integrals (A0, B0, C0, D0)
3. **NRGR-specific simplifications**: in the potential region (|q_0| << |q|), temporal and spatial loop momenta decouple

References:
- Goldberger & Rothstein hep-th/0409156, Sec 5 — 1-loop NRGR integrals
- Porto 1104.2712, Sec 3 — systematic loop calculation in NRGR
- Passarino & Veltman, Nucl. Phys. B 160 (1979) 151 — PV reduction
- Leibbrandt, Rev. Mod. Phys. 47 (1975) 849 — dim-reg review

## Design: MomentumIntegral type

```julia
# A single propagator denominator factor
struct PropagatorDenom
    momentum::TensorExpr     # e.g., q or q - k
    mass_sq::Any             # mass^2 (0 for graviton)
    power::Int               # exponent (1 for standard, higher for IBP)
end

# A loop integral
struct MomentumIntegral
    loop_momenta::Vector{Symbol}     # integration variables [:q] or [:q1, :q2]
    numerator::TensorExpr            # tensor-valued numerator N^{ab...}(q, k)
    denominators::Vector{PropagatorDenom}  # propagator factors
    dim::Any                         # spacetime dimension (4, or symbolic d = 4 - 2*eps)
    prefactor::Any                   # overall 1/(2pi)^d etc.
end

# Scalar master integrals (Passarino-Veltman basis)
struct ScalarIntegral
    topology::Symbol           # :A0, :B0, :C0, :D0 (tadpole, bubble, triangle, box)
    masses::Vector{Any}        # internal masses
    external_momenta::Vector{Any}  # external momentum invariants (k^2, (k1+k2)^2, ...)
    dim::Any                   # spacetime dimension
end
```

## Integration with existing infrastructure

Must build on:
- `TensorExpr` AST for numerator structure
- `contract_momenta` from `src/action/kernel_extraction.jl` — q_a q^a -> q^2
- `simplify` pipeline — simplification of numerator
- `contract_metrics` — eta^{ab} contraction in numerator
- `FeynmanDiagram` and `contract_diagram` from TGR-d42

## Function signatures

```julia
# Construct a momentum integral from a contracted diagram
function to_momentum_integral(contracted::TensorExpr,
                               loop_momenta::Vector{Symbol};
                               dim=4) -> MomentumIntegral

# d-dimensional metric contraction identity:
# eta^{ab} eta_{ab} = d, q^a q_a = q^2
function dimreg_contract(expr::TensorExpr; dim=:d) -> TensorExpr

# Tensor decomposition of loop momentum:
# int d^d q  q^a q^b f(q^2) = (eta^{ab} / d) int d^d q  q^2 f(q^2)
function tensor_reduce_1loop(integral::MomentumIntegral) -> MomentumIntegral

# Passarino-Veltman reduction to scalar master integrals
function pv_reduce(integral::MomentumIntegral) -> Vector{Tuple{Any, ScalarIntegral}}
# Returns: [(coefficient_1, I_1), (coefficient_2, I_2), ...]

# Identify the topology (number of propagators -> integral family)
function integral_topology(integral::MomentumIntegral) -> Symbol
# Returns :tadpole, :bubble, :triangle, :box, etc.

# Standard scalar integral results in dim-reg
function scalar_integral_result(si::ScalarIntegral) -> Any
# e.g., A0(m^2) = m^2 (1/eps - gamma + log(4pi) - log(m^2/mu^2) + 1)

# NRGR potential region: integrate over q_0 first, then |q|
function nrgr_potential_integral(integral::MomentumIntegral) -> MomentumIntegral
```

## Ground truth (verification tests)

1. **Dim-reg identity**: int d^d q  q^a q^b / (q^2)^n = (eta^{ab} / d) * int d^d q  q^2 / (q^2)^n.
   This is the fundamental tensor decomposition identity. Reference: Leibbrandt 1975 Eq 3.8.

2. **Topology identification**: A diagram with 2 propagators [q^2, (q-k)^2] is classified as :bubble (B0). A diagram with 3 propagators is :triangle (C0). A diagram with 4 is :box (D0).

3. **B0 result**: The bubble integral B0(k^2; 0, 0) = (1/eps) - gamma + log(4pi mu^2) - log(-k^2) in MS-bar. Reference: Passarino & Veltman 1979 Eq A.3.

4. **NRGR 1-loop**: The 1-loop graviton self-energy (bubble diagram with two 3-graviton vertices) produces a correction to the potential proportional to G^2 m1 m2 (m1 + m2) / r^2 at 1PN. Reference: Goldberger & Rothstein hep-th/0409156, Sec 5.

## Implementation notes

- Track d symbolically throughout; only substitute d = 4 - 2 eps at the end
- Use existing _sym_mul/_sym_div for symbolic arithmetic on coefficients
- For NRGR: the potential region simplification integrates q_0 via residues, leaving a 3d spatial integral over |q| which is a standard power-law integral
- Passarino-Veltman reduction is algebraic: express q^a in terms of external momenta k_i^a and contract with the numerator
- Start with stubs (topology identification + dim-reg identities), defer full PV reduction to a later issue
- ~100 LOC in src/feynman/loop_integrals.jl","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:31:46Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-15T10:48:39Z"}

old_value: {"id":"TGR-vdm.2","title":"Implement ADM decomposition for Hamiltonian formulation","description":"Implement ADM (Arnowitt-Deser-Misner) decomposition: g_{ab} -\u003e (N, N^i, gamma_{ij}) where N is lapse, N^i is shift vector, gamma_{ij} is spatial metric on spacelike hypersurface. Line element: ds^2 = -N^2 dt^2 + gamma_{ij}(dx^i + N^i dt)(dx^j + N^j dt). Canonical momenta: pi^{ij} = (sqrt(gamma)/2N)(K^{ij} - gamma^{ij}K) where K_{ij} = (1/2N)(dot{gamma}_{ij} - D_i N_j - D_j N_i) is extrinsic curvature. ADM Hamiltonian: H = integral (N H + N^i H_i) where H = (pi^{ij}pi_{ij} - (1/2)pi^2)/sqrt(gamma) - sqrt(gamma)R^{(3)} (Hamiltonian constraint) and H_i = -2 D_j pi^j_i (momentum constraint). Build on existing foliation infrastructure. New file: src/hamiltonian/adm.jl. Ground truth: Arnowitt, Deser, Misner (1962); Wald Ch 10. ~100 LOC.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:19:29Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:19:29Z"}

{"id":"TGR-bgl.6","title":"xPPN: Implement PPN field equations (order-by-order solving)","description":"Implement order-by-order solving of field equations in the PPN framework. Given a gravitational theory (action or field equations), solve for the metric order-by-order in v/c:
1. At O(2): solve for g₀₀^(2) and gᵢⱼ^(2) in terms of U
2. At O(3): solve for g₀ᵢ^(3) in terms of Vᵢ, Wᵢ
3. At O(4): solve for g₀₀^(4) in terms of Φ's
4. Read off PPN parameters from coefficients

Use existing solve_tensors infrastructure. Add to src/ppn/.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Ch 5.

Acceptance: ppn_solve(field_eqs, matter_source; order=4) returns PPNParams. Correct for GR field equations.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:14:00Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:14:00Z"}

{"id":"TGR-bgl.12","title":"xPPN: Implement PPN observable expressions (perihelion, deflection, Shapiro, Nordtvedt)","description":"Implement the standard PPN observables: closed-form expressions for solar system tests as functions of PPN parameters. These are the primary output of the PPN framework -- they connect abstract PPN parameters to measurable quantities.

Observables to implement (Will 2018, Ch 7-8):
1. Perihelion precession: delta_omega = (2+2gamma-beta) * 6pi M / a(1-e^2) per orbit
2. Light deflection: delta_theta = (1+gamma)/2 * 4M/d (impact parameter d)
3. Shapiro time delay: delta_t = (1+gamma) * 2M * ln(4 r_1 r_2 / d^2) 
4. Gravitational redshift: delta_nu/nu = (1+alpha) * Delta U / c^2
5. Nordtvedt effect: eta_N = 4beta - gamma - 3 - 10xi/3 - alpha_1 + 2alpha_2/3 - 2zeta_1/3 - zeta_2/3
6. Geodetic precession: Omega_gp = (gamma + 1/2) * (M/r) per orbit (de Sitter/LT)
7. Preferred-frame effects: orbital polarization ~ alpha_1, alpha_2, alpha_3

API:
- ppn_perihelion(params; M, a, e) -> symbolic expression
- ppn_deflection(params; M, d) -> symbolic expression
- ppn_shapiro_delay(params; M, r1, r2, d) -> symbolic expression
- ppn_nordtvedt_eta(params) -> scalar expression in PPN parameters
- ppn_geodetic_precession(params; M, r) -> symbolic expression
- ppn_preferred_frame_bounds(params) -> Dict of alpha constraints

Design constraints:
- Expressions are closed-form functions of PPNParams, NOT from solving field equations
- These are SEPARATE from the field equation solving -- they parametrize observables
- The Nordtvedt parameter eta = 4beta - gamma - 3 - ... is a derived quantity
- Must return symbolic expressions (TScalar with symbolic content)
- Solar system bounds stored as metadata: gamma-1 < 2.3e-5 (Cassini), beta-1 < 8e-5 (LLR), etc.

Add to src/ppn/observables.jl.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Table 7.1, Ch 7-8; Will, Living Rev. Rel. 17, 4 (2014) Tables 3-4.

Acceptance:
- ppn_nordtvedt_eta(PPNParams(:GR)) == 0
- ppn_perihelion expression contains the combination (2+2gamma-beta)
- ppn_deflection expression contains the combination (1+gamma)/2
- All observables reduce to GR predictions when PPNParams() is substituted","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-15T16:20:32Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-15T16:20:32Z"}

{"id":"TGR-dai.2","title":"Implement GammaMatrix type with Clifford algebra contraction","description":"Implement GammaMatrix type representing γ^a with a spacetime index. Core Clifford algebra contraction rule: {γ^a, γ^b} = 2g^{ab}. This means γ^a γ^b + γ^b γ^a = 2g^{ab}·I where I is the identity in spinor space. New struct GammaMatrix \u003c: TensorExpr with spinor indices (suppressed) and one spacetime index. Implement gamma_chain_simplify that applies Clifford relation to reduce products. New file: src/fermions/gamma.jl. Ground truth: Wald GR Appendix B. ~100 LOC.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:12:00Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:12:00Z"}

{"id":"TGR-swh.2","title":"Implement general affine connection","description":"Implement general affine connection Gamma^a_{bc} without metric compatibility or torsion-free assumptions. API: define_affine_connection!(reg, :Gamma; manifold=:M4, metric=:g) that registers a connection tensor with 3 indices (1 up, 2 down) and NO assumed symmetry in lower indices (unlike Levi-Civita which has Gamma^a_{(bc)}). The connection defines a covariant derivative: nabla_a V^b = partial_a V^b + Gamma^b_{ac} V^c. Must also register Levi-Civita part Gamma_LC and distortion N = Gamma - Gamma_LC as derived tensors. New file: src/metric_affine/connection.jl. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Sec 2. ~100 LOC.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:16:23Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:16:23Z"}

old_value: {"id":"TGR-vdm.3","title":"Implement Poisson bracket for tensor fields","description":"Implement Poisson bracket {f, g} for functionals of tensor fields on a spatial slice. For canonical pair (gamma_{ij}(x), pi^{kl}(y)): {gamma_{ij}(x), pi^{kl}(y)} = (1/2)(delta^k_i delta^l_j + delta^k_j delta^l_i) delta^3(x-y). General Poisson bracket: {F, G} = integral d^3x (delta F/delta gamma_{ij} delta G/delta pi^{ij} - delta F/delta pi^{ij} delta G/delta gamma_{ij}). Must handle: (1) functional derivatives of tensor expressions, (2) smeared constraints (integrated with test functions), (3) Poisson bracket algebra (Jacobi identity). {"id":"TGR-wq0.3","title":"Implement Hassan-Rosen interaction potential","description":"Implement Hassan-Rosen bimetric interaction potential: V(g,f) = m^2 Sigma_{n=0}^{4} beta_n e_n(S) where S^a_b = (sqrt(g^{-1}f))^a_b is the matrix square root and e_n are elementary symmetric polynomials. Explicitly: e_0(S) = 1, e_1(S) = Tr(S) = S^a_a, e_2(S) = (1/2)((Tr S)^2 - Tr(S^2)), e_3(S) = (1/6)((Tr S)^3 - 3 Tr S Tr(S^2) + 2 Tr(S^3)), e_4(S) = det(S). The 5 parameters beta_0...beta_4 plus m^2 define the theory. Implement: hassan_rosen_potential(g, f, params) -\u003e TensorExpr. New file: src/bimetric/potential.jl. Ground truth: Hassan \u0026 Rosen (2012) Eq 2.4; de Rham, Living Rev. Rel. 17, 7 (2014) Sec 8. ~100 LOC.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:18:23Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:18:23Z"}

{"id":"TGR-swh.3","title":"Implement torsion tensor with irreducible decomposition","description":"Implement torsion tensor T^a_{bc} = 2 Gamma^a_{[bc]} = Gamma^a_{bc} - Gamma^a_{cb} (antisymmetric in lower indices). Irreducible decomposition (d=4): T^a_{bc} = (1/3)(T_b delta^a_c - T_c delta^a_b) + (1/3)epsilon^a_{bcd} S^d + q^a_{bc} where T_b = T^a_{ba} is the torsion vector trace, S^a = (1/6)epsilon^{abcd}T_{bcd} is the axial (pseudo-trace) vector, and q^a_{bc} is the tensor part (traceless, no axial part). Implement: (1) torsion_tensor(connection) -\u003e TensorExpr, (2) torsion_vector(T), torsion_axial(T), torsion_tensor_part(T). New file: src/metric_affine/torsion.jl. {"id":"TGR-dai.3","title":"Implement gamma matrix trace identities","description":"Implement trace identities for gamma matrix chains. Core identities: Tr(I) = 4, Tr(γ^a) = 0, Tr(γ^a γ^b) = 4g^{ab}, Tr(γ^a γ^b γ^c) = 0, Tr(γ^a γ^b γ^c γ^d) = 4(g^{ab}g^{cd} - g^{ac}g^{bd} + g^{ad}g^{bc}). {"id":"TGR-wt5","title":"GHP: Implement spin/boost weight system","description":"Implement the GHP (Geroch-Held-Penrose) spin and boost weight system. Every GHP quantity has a pair of weights (p,q) under the rescaling l-\u003elambda*l, n-\u003elambda^{-1}*n, m-\u003ee^{i*theta}*m. A quantity eta has type {p,q} if eta -\u003e lambda^{(p+q)/2} e^{i*(p-q)/2 theta} eta. Examples: Psi_0 has type {4,0}, rho has type {1,1}, sigma has type {3,-1}. Implement as metadata/trait on NP scalars. Struct: GHPWeight(p::Int, q::Int). Function: ghp_weight(scalar) -\u003e GHPWeight. ~60 LOC in src/spinors/ghp.jl. Reference: GHP (1973) Section 2. Test: (1) verify weights of all 12 spin coefficients match Table 1 of GHP, (2) Psi_0 weight = {4,0}, Psi_4 weight = {-4,0}, (3) product rule: weight of product = sum of weights.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:11:20Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:11:20Z"}
